input.code: const q = `SELECT * FROM users WHERE email='${email}' AND pass='${pass}'`; db.query(q);

findings.risk_summary: SQL query is built by string interpolation from untrusted input.
findings.fix_recommendation: Use parameterized queries/prepared statements and validate input.
findings.evidence: Interpolated email/pass directly into SQL string.

input.code: router.get('/orders/:id', async (req,res)=>{ const order = await db.order.findByPk(req.params.id); res.json(order); });

findings.risk_summary: Route fetches object by user-controlled id without ownership authorization check.
findings.fix_recommendation: Enforce ownership or role checks before returning object data.

findings.risk_summary: Deprecated/insecure cipher mode can expose plaintext patterns.
findings.fix_recommendation: Use authenticated encryption mode (e.g., AES-GCM) with proper nonce management.

input.code: const sql = 'SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = ? AND password = ?'; db.query(sql, [email, password]);
router.get('/orders/:id', async (req,res)=>{ const order = await db.order.findByPk(req.params.id); if (!order || order.userId !== req.user.id) return res.sendStatus(403); res.json(order); });
const criteria = req.query.q; models.sequelize.query(`SELECT * FROM Products WHERE name LIKE '%${criteria}%'`);

findings.risk_summary: User-controlled query input is directly interpolated into a raw SQL string, enabling SQL injection.
findings.fix_recommendation: Use parameterized queries/bind parameters instead of string interpolation in the SQL statement.
findings.evidence: Raw SQL query contains `${criteria}` derived from req.query.

input.code: const userInput = req.params.username; const code = userInput.replace(/'/g, ""); const value = eval(code);

findings.risk_summary: User-controlled data flows into eval(), allowing code injection/arbitrary code execution.
findings.fix_recommendation: Remove eval on user input and replace with safe parsing/validation logic.
findings.evidence: eval(code) executes value derived from req.params.username.

findings.risk_summary: Directory exposure is publicly mounted with listing/index behavior and no access restriction, enabling unauthorized browsing of uploaded content.
findings.fix_recommendation: Disable directory listing/indexing and enforce authentication/authorization middleware before this mount.
findings.evidence: Public express.static mount exposes uploads path with index enabled and no auth guard.
